trade.summary: Russia exports commodities, gold, diamonds, and some manufactured goods to the UAE. The UAE exports refined goods and re-exports to Russia. Dubai has become a major hub for Russian capital flight since 2022 sanctions. Russian gold is traded through Dubai markets. The UAE is not participating in Western sanctions against Russia.

diplomatic.summary: The UAE has maintained a studied neutrality on the Russia-Ukraine war, abstaining in key UN votes on Russian invasion condemnation. UAE has not joined Western sanctions. Dubai has become a major destination for Russian capital and wealthy Russians. The UAE engages Russia through OPEC+ coordination. This neutrality creates diplomatic tension with Western partners.
recent_incidents: UAE abstained on UN resolutions condemning Russia's invasion of Ukraine

military.summary: Military ties are limited and have not grown significantly. UAE relies on Western defense partnerships for its security architecture. Some historical Russian arms sales to the UAE in the 1990s-2000s but US/European dominance in recent decades. Russia and UAE interact on Libya and Syria issues.

financial.summary: Dubai has emerged as a major financial refuge for Russian capital since 2022 Western sanctions. Russian HNWI (high net worth individuals) have poured billions into Dubai real estate and financial assets. Russian banks have UAE operations. This creates financial benefit for UAE but geopolitical risk with Western partners.

energy.summary: UAE and Russia coordinate within OPEC+ on production quotas, a key strategic alignment. Both are major oil producers and interact on global energy market management. Some Russian gas/oil now transits through UAE routes to third markets.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation is limited. Some historical educational exchanges have declined post-2022. Russian tech talent has relocated to UAE, creating an informal knowledge transfer.
